title: [web-review/P2] factorylm.com — 3,335 KiB cache savings; static assets missing long-lived Cache-Control
bodyExcerpt: **Route:** / (nginx site-wide) **Audit date:** 2026-05-08  **Evidence:** Lighthouse cache-insight estimated savings 3,335 KiB; repeat visitors re-download full payload on every visit.  **Fix:** Add `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` for fingerprinted static assets in nginx config. Use shorter TTL (e.g. `max-age=3600`) for HTML files.  GitHub issue: [https://github.com/Mikecranesync/MIRA/issues/1096](<https://github.com/Mikecranesync/MIRA/issues/1096>)

title: [web-review/P2] factorylm.com / — render-blocking resources add 310ms to first paint
bodyExcerpt: **Route:** / **Audit date:** 2026-05-08  **Evidence:** Lighthouse render-blocking-resources insight estimated savings 310ms to first paint.  **Impact:** 310ms added to First Contentful Paint and LCP. Every 100ms delay costs \~1% conversion rate.  **Fix:**  * Add `defer` / `async` attributes on non-critical `<script>` tags * Inline critical CSS above the fold; load full stylesheet asynchronously * Use `<link rel="preload">` for key resources  Git… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

title: [web-review/P2] app.factorylm.com / — 3-hop redirect chain: / → /feed/ → /login?callbackUrl → /login/
bodyExcerpt: curl -sIL on [https://app.factorylm.com/](<https://app.factorylm.com/>) reports 3 hops before reaching the final URL [https://app.factorylm.com/login/?callbackUrl=%2Ffeed%2F](<https://app.factorylm.com/login/?callbackUrl=%2Ffeed%2F>).  Chain: nginx 301 → Next.js 307 → Next.js 308.  Extra hops add latency and confuse crawlers.  **Fix:** Collapse to a single 302 redirect from `/` → `/login/` at the nginx level, removing the intermediate `/feed/` s… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

title: [web-review/P2] app.factorylm.com — CSP allows unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval in script-src, negating XSS protection

title: P0: Pin mira-hub:latest in docker-compose.hub.yml
bodyExcerpt: ## Problem  `docker-compose.hub.yml:6` uses `image: mira-hub:latest`. Breaks reproducibility — a stale or bad build can be pulled silently.  ## Fix  Pin to SemVer (`v3.4.0` from VERSION file) or commit-SHA. Push CI to dual-tag.  ```diff -    image: mira-hub:latest +    image: mira-hub:v3.4.0 ```  Also pin `RAG_EMBEDDING_MODEL=nomic-embed-text:latest` in mira-core compose.  ## Cross-refs  * GitHub: [https://github.com/Mikecranesync/MIRA/issues/11… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

title: P0: Hardcoded Bravo LAN IP (192.168.1.11) in mira-hub asset-intelligence.ts
bodyExcerpt: ## Problem  `mira-hub/src/lib/agents/asset-intelligence.ts:103`  ```ts const base = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL ?? "http://192.168.1.11:11434"; ```  Bakes a private LAN IP (Bravo) into a code path that ships to SaaS. If `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` is unset on prod, the call silently targets a private IP.  ## Fix  Throw on missing env var, OR use a service DNS name:  ```ts const base = process.env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL; if (!base) throw new Error("OLLAMA_BASE_UR… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

title: P1: Docker/nginx hardening bundle — root containers, rate limiting, healthchecks, weak defaults
bodyExcerpt: ## 7 hardening items  ### Security  1. **5 services run as root** — add `USER mira` to Dockerfiles for: mira-pipeline, mira-mcp, mira-ingest, mira-relay, mira-sidecar. Pattern from mira-hub. 2. **No nginx rate limiting** — add `limit_req_zone` in `nginx-oracle.conf` + `nginx-phase2-live.conf`: `/v1/` 10 req/s, `/api/ingest/` 5 req/s, `/api/agents/` 10 req/s. 3. **Weak Postgres fallback password** — `docker-compose.saas.yml:339,363` has `:-nango`… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

title: P0: Deploy hardened read-only WebDev endpoints (tags / alarms / tagHistory)
bodyExcerpt: ## Why  Upstream WhiskeyHouse WebDev handlers include write paths (tag write, alarm acknowledge, tag delete, `scriptExec`) that present unacceptable risk for a single-tenant production gateway. Forking to read-only + adding a tag-path whitelist removes the dangerous code paths physically — even if MCP credentials leak, the endpoints cannot write or be used to enumerate the full tag tree.  Demo narrative needs read-only access to live tag values … (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

Append to `C:\Users\hharp\Documents\ignition-mcp\.env`:     ```    IGNITION_MCP_USERNAME=mcp-readonly    IGNITION_MCP_PASSWORD=<from 1Password>    IGNITION_MCP_WEBDEV_TAG_ENDPOINT=https://localhost:8043/system/webdev/Global/GatewayAPI/tags    IGNITION_MCP… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

title: feat(schema): Add external ID columns to cmms_equipment for i3X interoperability
bodyExcerpt: ## Context  CESMII's i3X (Industrial Information Interoperability eXchange) is an open, vendor-agnostic REST API spec for accessing contextualized manufacturing data. Working group includes Rockwell, Inductive Automation, Siemens, AWS, Microsoft.  MIRA's architecture is \~38% aligned with i3X (UNS browse API, KG schema, multi-hop traversal). The biggest gap collapsing the portability/interoperability claim is: **no external ID columns anywhere**… (truncated, use `get_issue` for full description)

title: [web-review/P1] factorylm.com / — SVG console error: rect attribute rx: Expected length fires on every load
bodyExcerpt: **Route:** / **Audit date:** 2026-05-08  **Evidence:** Playwright console capture: `Error: <rect> attribute rx: Expected length, "0 0 12 12"` — Lighthouse errors-in-console audit score 0  **Fix:** Find SVG element using `rx="0 0 12 12"` (malformed — rx takes a single length, not a rect shorthand) and replace with `rx="0"` or remove the attribute.  GitHub issue: [https://github.com/Mikecranesync/MIRA/issues/1094](<https://github.com/Mikecranesync/MIRA/issues/1094>)
